races.name: Weatherbys nhstallions.co.uk EBF Junior "National Hunt" Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mollys Lad
runners.spotlight: Just ahead of Crack Ops in a Cheltenham bumper; third to a good horse (ran well in the Fred Winter) in his first race over hurdles and, although beaten at odds of 4-7 at Carlisle, that winner could also be useful (unbeaten and unexposed); in the mix.
runners.comment: Has come up against promising opposition in both his races over hurdles

runners.name: Planters Punch
runners.spotlight: Raced prominently and hung on narrowly for a successful debut at Hereford in November (good ground) and the clear second has won all his three races since then; has to be of strong interest even with a penalty.
runners.comment: The form of his Hereford win has worked out well so has to be of strong interest

runners.name: Crack Ops
runners.spotlight: Useful bumper form on good ground, winning at Ludlow and sixth of 16 at Cheltenham (Listed); the ground was good to soft when a weak finisher on hurdle debut at Catterick; back on a drier surface and with headgear tried, he should fare better.
runners.comment: Useful bumper form on good ground; softer surface perhaps no help in first hurdle race

runners.name: Saxons Pride
runners.spotlight: It's not strong form but she showed ability in her two bumpers and the dam was a winning hurdler; a 4yo filly receiving all the allowances.
runners.comment: It's not strong form but she showed ability in her two bumpers; dam won over hurdles

runners.name: Masterfield
runners.spotlight: Ran okay in bumpers but was a long way behind Molly's Lad in a 4yo hurdle at Carlisle.
runners.comment: Ran okay in bumpers but was a long way behind Molly's Lad in a 4yo hurdle at Carlisle

runners.name: Pippins Legend
runners.spotlight: Had a wind operation since pulling up in his second run over hurdles (raced freely); some promise prior to that but he needs to take a marked step forward to be winning this.
runners.comment: Had a wind operation but needs to take a marked step forward to be winning this

runners.name: Mistral St Georges
runners.spotlight: 50-1 for last month's debut at Newbury (2m, good to soft) but very much in contention before falling heavily at penultimate flight; has possibilities if that experience did not dent his confidence.
runners.comment: In the process of running well when falling two out on Newbury debut

races.name: Xenon Workplace Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Coumeenoole
runners.spotlight: 12-race ex-Irish maiden who hasn't yet shown any benefit for joining Dan Skelton, in handicap, novice and maiden company (1m7f to 2m5f; all on good to soft); did win his point on good ground, so conditions could be more favourable here, and he stayed 3m in Ireland; with all that in mind, he's considered.
runners.comment: Yet to show any benefit for joining Dan Skelton but that could all change here

runners.name: Parliamo
runners.spotlight: Definite improvement when beaten about 13l into third in a soft-ground maiden at Hereford (2m3f) and the front two that day have progressed to better things; this gelding might do likewise now sent further for his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Improved third at Hereford and a likely improver now up in trip for handicap debut

runners.name: Slim Marvel
runners.spotlight: Had plenty of racing in Ireland where she was effective on good ground; conditions were heavy when going close at Ffos Las a month ago (3m) and she travelled strongly deep into that race; possibilities off 4lb higher.
runners.comment: Just missed out over 3m at Ffos Las and she travelled strongly deep into that race

runners.name: Woodview Lad
runners.spotlight: Didn't last long with Gordon Elliott; 100-1 for handicap debut at Stratford (2m) and never got involved; raised in trip.
runners.comment: 100-1 for handicap debut at Stratford (2m) and never got involved; raised in trip

runners.name: Escortkheops
runners.spotlight: Entitled to win his maiden at Plumpton last time (2m4f, good to soft) and unlikely that represented significant improvement, if any; however, has shown he can be competitive off this mark so has to be on the radar.
runners.comment: Recent maiden winner who has shown he can be competitive off this mark

runners.name: Geordie Night
runners.spotlight: More exposed than a few of his rivals and he's only 1-13 over hurdles; his third at Doncaster is nothing to shout about.
runners.comment: More exposed than a few of his rivals and he's only 1-13 over hurdles

runners.name: Daly Boy
runners.spotlight: Has regressed since chasing home a class act on hurdle debut at Sedgefield and finished weakly over 3m at Haydock last time after a wind operation; others safer.
runners.comment: Finished weakly over 3m at Haydock last time after a wind operation; others safer

runners.name: Ocean Walz
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; the second at Ludlow in February (2m5f, good to soft) offers hope but pretty much tailed off on either side of that.
runners.comment: Ludlow second offers hope but pretty much tailed off on either side of that

runners.name: Golden Prince
runners.spotlight: Not really bred for this huge rise in trip but the form of his third in a 3m point has worked out well so there's hope that he could display improvement on this handicap debut; market needs checking.
runners.comment: Not bred for this rise in trip but the form of his third in a 3m point has worked out well

races.name: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing Mares' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jollie Dame
runners.spotlight: Form figures may look encouraging but she's been beaten between 16l and 22l in her last three races, the latest on hurdle debut at Kempton (2m, good; 12-1); has to improve to beat the best of these.
runners.comment: Form figures look good but beaten between 16l and 22l in her last three races

runners.name: Halftheworldaway
runners.spotlight: Bought for 130,000euros after finishing second in an Irish point; it's been a solid start under rules and it was all very easy from the front here last time; all her runs have been on soft ground so that's a grey area, but has strong form claims.
runners.comment: All her runs have been on soft ground so that's a grey area, but strong form claims

runners.name: Roses All The Way
runners.spotlight: Listed form in bumpers and over hurdles; didn't have to improve to win at Musselburgh (2m4f, good to soft; odds-on) and came up short under a penalty at Ayr (first-time cheekpieces); should give her running and entitled to be involved.
runners.comment: Beaten under a penalty latest but should give her running and be involved

runners.name: Highland Haven
runners.spotlight: Best form has been in bumpers, the pick on soft ground when ahead of Roses All The Way; beaten about 6l in two novice hurdles so her turn will come; respected.
runners.comment: Beaten about 6l in two novice hurdles so her turn will come; respected

runners.name: After The Ball
runners.spotlight: Unable to win any of her eight points in Ireland and looks the yard second string here.
runners.comment: Unable to win any of her eight points in Ireland and looks the yard second string here

runners.name: She Can Run
runners.spotlight: First foal out of a useful 2m3f-3m6f hurdle/chase winner but likely to be vulnerable with no experience behind her.
runners.comment: First foal out of a useful 2m3f-3m6f hurdle/chase winner; has no experience

runners.name: Seekingrainbows
runners.spotlight: 300-1 when tailed off behind Halftheworldaway here a month ago.
runners.comment: 300-1 when tailed off behind Halftheworldaway here a month ago
runners.owner: S L Edwards & S A Hollinshead

races.name: HRS Cladding Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Busty Boy
runners.spotlight: Sole win under rules came in a handicap chase in 2023 (2m7f, soft); stays 3m6f but nearly won over this far at Carlisle on his reappearance and not disgraced since.
runners.comment: Nearly won over this far at Carlisle on his reappearance and not disgraced since

runners.name: Doyouknowwhatimean
runners.spotlight: Ex-Dan Skelton; he has pulled up in his three of his last four races but did go close when he did get round at Uttoxeter in December (2m4f, heavy; effective on good); he's risky but also capable.
runners.comment: Form figures speak for themselves; he's risky but also capable, as he showed in December

runners.name: The Good Doctor
runners.spotlight: Potentially well handicapped but he's 0-7 over fences and has been well below his best the last twice, on good ground (tried a visor) and soft (no headgear); did win in a tongue-tie when trained in France and that aid belatedly returns; couldn't rule out.
runners.comment: Poor the last twice but has the form to feature and a tongue-tie could be significant

runners.name: Anyharminasking
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner who is 0-4 over fences, with only effort of note in discipline when beaten in a match at Warwick in October (2m4f); does seem happier on good ground than soft, so last two efforts could be excused; cheekpieces return after going without them last time.
runners.comment: Does seem happier on good ground than soft, so last two efforts could be excused

runners.name: Lady Jago
runners.spotlight: Won three small-field chases at up to 3m1f for Dan Skelton; bought for Â£10,000 in July and hasn't recaptured her best form for new yard.
runners.comment: Bought for Â£10,000 in July and hasn't recaptured her best form for new yard

runners.name: Palamon
runners.spotlight: Maiden chaser and with an overall strike-rate of 2-32; he hasn't got anything going over fences for current yard but he's dropped sharply in the weights and getting back on good ground can only help; would be interesting if the market offered encouragement.
runners.comment: Nothing for current yard but off a reduced mark here and he's back on good ground

runners.name: Rule Me In
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles but no closer than 25l in five runs over fences and he's hard to trust at the minute.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles but no closer than 25l in five runs over fences and he's hard to trust

runners.name: Bleu dEnfer
runners.spotlight: Out of the handicap when a respectable fourth in a Class 2 at Musselburgh on his penultimate start (2m4f, good to soft) and has had wind surgery since finishing tailed off at Kelso; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Chance on his penultimate effort and had wind surgery since tailed off last time

runners.name: Nativehill
runners.spotlight: Fetched Â£260,000 after winning his Irish point but left Nicky Henderson for Â£45,000 last May after pulling up in the Grade 1 Albert Bartlett at the Cheltenham Festival; showed plenty of promise before then and he's still lightly raced; market could be informative ahead of this chase/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Last seen pulling up in a hurdle at 2025 Cheltenham Festival; lightly raced; new yard

runners.name: Kahavari
runners.spotlight: Without a win since his French hurdle honours in 2024; however, has finished runner-up in two of his last three races and should run well if coping with the good ground which would be a first for him.
runners.comment: Should run well if coping with the good ground which would be a first for him

races.name: Uncommon Practice Handicap Chase

runners.name: I See The Sea
runners.spotlight: Only 1lb higher than for his Catterick win in November (3m1f, good) and went close back there in January; does have his off days (finished last when third on final run) but there's a big run in him if he fancies it.
runners.comment: Has his share of off days but there's a big run in him if he fancies it

runners.name: Felton Bellevue
runners.spotlight: None too consistent but he has won five times over fences and nearly made that six last time at Plumpton (3m1f, good to soft); he's handicapped about right but needs considering.
runners.comment: Inconsistent but nearly won for a sixth time over fences on final outing at Plumpton
runners.owner: Mrs H Munn R E Good B Case

runners.name: Subtle Fortune
runners.spotlight: Her hurdle win was on soft and the chase win at Hereford two runs back on heavy; had luck on her side that day and was then beaten 10l off this mark at Leicester (good to soft); however, at least she's a recent winner and did win a point on good ground.
runners.comment: Had luck on her side when winning at Hereford; beaten 10l the last time

runners.name: Good Work
runners.spotlight: Extremely hard to win with, having won only one of his 34 races over fences and runner-up on 13 of those occasions; even if he jumps better than last time (beaten 18l), he'll probably find a way to come up short; a rare run without any form of headgear.
runners.comment: Extremely hard to win with, having won only one of his 34 races over fences

runners.name: Nine Nine Nine
runners.spotlight: Effective on all ground but Leicester is his favourite patch; his record elsewhere isn't that solid and twice well held here, as was also the case when tried in blinkers last time at Uttoxeter.
runners.comment: Leicester his favourite patch; his record elsewhere isn't that solid; remote 2nd last time

runners.name: The Scorpion King
runners.spotlight: Only four completed when a 16l third at Exeter (3m, good to soft) and that was another effort well below the form he showed in 2024; reduced mark is for good reason but this isn't a strong race, so hard to leave him out of calculations.
runners.comment: Only four completed when a 16l third at Exeter and remains well below his best

runners.name: Streamsforth Lad
runners.spotlight: Maiden; three times third over hurdles; 0-4 over fences and yet to complete the course, yet he was bang in contention when falling two out at Stratford three weeks ago (2m6f, soft; has form on good); if avoiding the mistakes he should have a role to play.
runners.comment: Bang in contention when falling two out at Stratford three weeks ago

runners.name: Pearl Island
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-14 for Henry Daly, the win coming in a 3m handicap chase at Uttoxeter in late 2024 (in first-time blinkers that were retained until now); has lost his way but still only a 7yo and he's on a fair mark for this yard debut; may prefer slower ground, however.
runners.comment: Has lost his way but still only a 7yo and he's on a fair mark for this yard debut

runners.name: Three Zambezis
runners.spotlight: Hardly brings compelling form claims and heavy ground wasn't an obvious excuse when a remote fourth at Hereford on chase debut (2m5f); essentially disappointing given the promise of his point runs.
runners.comment: 0-9 under rules and essentially disappointing given the promise of his point runs

runners.name: New Invention
runners.spotlight: Forgettable form over hurdles, including three handicaps from 2m to 3m; there's a chance he could turn out better over fences but a watching brief seems best on this first attempt.
runners.comment: Forgettable form over hurdles, including three handicaps from 2m to 3m; chase debut

races.name: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Service Minimum
runners.spotlight: Both her novice wins came on good ground and she'll appreciate a return to such conditions after running fairly well on soft last time at Market Rasen (2m4f); considered off her lowest mark in a handicap.
runners.comment: Both wins on good ground and latest run on soft wasn't too bad an effort

runners.name: Voodoo Angel
runners.spotlight: Now a 12-race maiden but as consistent as her form figures would suggest; however, her best form in Britain has been over shorter and she has tried this far.
runners.comment: Consistent but her best form in Britain has been over shorter and she has tried this far

runners.name: Roaring Home
runners.spotlight: Consistent this season if forgiven her penultimate blip, after which she underwent wind surgery; it was a respectable effort at Haydock next time as that was a competitive Class 2 handicap (2m5f); goes well on good ground and looks a big player.
runners.comment: Mostly consistent; after a wind op she ran well in a competitive Haydock handicap

runners.name: Morning Air
runners.spotlight: Won a heavy-ground maiden hurdle at Leicester on British/hurdles debut in late 2024; lightly raced since; ran quite well in the 2025 Chester Cup but has offered little back over hurdles following a break of 226 days; now goes in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Offered little back over hurdles following a break of 226 days; now goes in cheekpieces
runners.owner: Lady Price C Shaw C Boylan & I Tagg

runners.name: Eastern Shores
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and has improved for the fitting of this headgear in her last three races, the latest her handicap debut (beaten 5.5l); could have more to offer over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and has improved for the fitting of this headgear in her last three races

runners.name: Nevershedatear
runners.spotlight: Point winner; quiet in novices and had lost her position before the saddle slipped on handicap debut at Sedgefield (2m4f, good; 14-1); now tries a hood.
runners.comment: Had lost her position before saddle slipped on handicap debut at Sedgefield; now hooded

runners.name: Rock On Cassie
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle for Gordon Elliott in 2022 but steadily on the decline ever since; no worthwhile form for current yard.
runners.comment: Won a maiden hurdle for Gordon Elliott in 2022 but steadily on the decline ever since

races.name: Equine Products NH Trainers League Novices' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jehol De Thaix
runners.spotlight: Perked up again sent handicapping for his two starts this season, in fact best effort when second of nine at Haydock (2m5f, good to soft; up 3lb today) last time; on that evidence this longer trip will suit him well.
runners.comment: Second at Haydock (2m5f) last time suggested that this longer trip will suit him well

runners.name: Milan Park
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten trying 3m on penultimate outing; best form on starts either side when taking minor handicap honours at Wetherby (2m4f, soft) and Kelso (extended 2m6f, heavy) and those give him place claims.
runners.comment: Minor handicap honours over 2m4f and an extended 2m6f give him place claims

runners.name: Dramatic License
runners.spotlight: Won maiden at Downpatrick (2m6f, good) last August; tough task in Listed novice in October on final run for Stuart Crawford, before sold for Â£26,000 and tailing off when 150-1 for stable/handicap debut at Wetherby (3m, good to soft) two weeks ago; may have needed that run but a dramatic turnaround is needed.
runners.comment: Tailed off when 150-1 for yard/handicap debut at Wetherby (3m, good to soft) two weeks ago

runners.name: Snowrocco
runners.spotlight: Won easily this winter over about 3m on good to soft at Newcastle and Musselburgh; fourth off revised mark at Market Rasen (2m4f, soft) was another good effort but tailed off at Musselburgh 11 days ago when most of the hurdles were omitted; needs to resume improvement in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Twice won easily over about 3m this winter; tailed off 11 days ago and now has cheekpieces

runners.name: Tamarind Bay
runners.spotlight: Stacks of minor honours and his second in a Doncaster maiden last time, after 101 days off, was one of his best efforts; another was in November on his only go at about 2m6f; the lack of a win is a touch disconcerting, though.
runners.comment: Stacks of minor honours; the lack of a win is a touch disconcerting, though

runners.name: Chemoulin
runners.spotlight: Showed ability on first two starts (here on second occasion) before sticking at it to grab a 2l win in maiden at Haydock (2m5f, good to soft) 26 days ago; that win came only after a wide-margin leader fell two out but this 6yo shaped nonetheless as though he has a future as a stayer; with further improvement likely, therefore, he's in with a shot on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Lucky to win a 2m5f Haydock maiden but shaped as though he has a future as a stayer

runners.name: Tea Boy
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on debut in a bumper at Hexham last March but unable to reproduce that form in a further two starts in bumpers and three over hurdles; this is his handicap debut, however, and he's well related.
runners.comment: Best effort was debut bumper; this is his handicap debut, however, and he's well related

runners.name: Nosy Neighbour
runners.spotlight: Promising 19l third on hurdle debut at Southwell (2m, good to soft) in November; tailed off both starts since (in quick succession last month) but this is a whopping step up in trip for his handicap debut and his dam was a pretty useful 2m-3m winner, with stamina in the family; needs to brush up his jumping but is very much one to note.
runners.comment: Step up in trip on handicap debut and there's stamina in the family; very much one to note

races.name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: I C U In My Dreams
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who made the frame in bumpers at Catterick and Newcastle, before her 12l second of six in a maiden at Doncaster (2m, good to soft; set steady pace) 18 days ago on hurdle debut; while not that exciting in most contexts, none of today's rivals have accomplished more.
runners.comment: 12l second of six in Doncaster maiden (2m, good to soft; set steady pace) on hurdle debut

runners.name: Lipstick Traces
runners.spotlight: Second or third in three bumpers and modest form in two of her three hurdle races, including in a hood on latest outing; the lesser hurdles effort was on her one attempt at this sort of trip, though, and overall improvement should be required anyway.
runners.comment: Second or third in three bumpers and modest form in two of her three hurdle races

runners.name: Curious Mrs Fox
runners.spotlight: 25-race maiden on Flat who mostly struggled last season.
runners.comment: 25-race maiden on Flat who mostly struggled last season

runners.name: R Krista
runners.spotlight: Easily closest finish in bumpers came at Sedgefield (soft) on second start but she still trailed home in last of six; best watched on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Easily closest finish in bumpers was at Sedgefield on second start, albeit last of six

runners.name: Lady Lucky
runners.spotlight: 250-1 and 200-1 when pulled up at Catterick (2m3f).
runners.comment: 250-1 and 200-1 when pulled up at Catterick (2m3f)

runners.name: Valette
runners.spotlight: 19l fourth of five in novice at Sedgefield (2m1f, soft) in November and 100-1 when pulled up at Fairyhouse (2m4f, soft) in January; sold for Â£1,800 two weeks later and lacks persuasive claims but this looks a very weak race.
runners.comment: Fourth of five at Sedgefield (2m1f) and pulled up at Fairyhouse (2m4f); sold for Â£1,800

runners.name: Royal Maggs
runners.spotlight: 2-3 in British points; low-level achievement, however, in her one bumper and two runs over hurdles; hooded on last two starts but raced freely.
runners.comment: 2-3 in British points; low-level achievement in her one bumper and two runs over hurdles
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing S Knowles & Partners

runners.name: Dame In Disguise
runners.spotlight: Has shown a little ability in her four bumpers but needs to show a lot more; bred for better.
runners.comment: Has shown a little ability in her four bumpers but needs to show a lot more

races.name: Cinch Self Storage cinchstorage.co.uk Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Florita
runners.spotlight: A dual runner-up in points, she cut little ice over hurdles but found her mark over fences, winning twice over an extended 2m3f at Market Rasen (good to soft/soft); flopped, however, as favourite trying 3m at that track six weeks ago.
runners.comment: Won twice over an extended 2m3f at Market Rasen; flopped trying 3m there six weeks ago

runners.name: Cahiers Den
runners.spotlight: Maiden point winner who is 0-9 under rules; however, best form when switched to chasing for handicaps at Uttoxeter (2m4f, soft) and Wetherby (3m, soft) on last two outings and he may well be on the premises.
runners.comment: Best form with switch to chasing for handicaps last two outings; could be on the premises

runners.name: Sir Carnegie
runners.spotlight: Maiden prior to winning from off the pace in novice handicap chases over C&D (good) and at Kelso (2m7f, good to soft) in the autumn; not so good latest start and has had four months off since; big player if back to best.
runners.comment: Won twice (once C&D) before a lesser show four months back; big player if back to best

runners.name: Aill Dubh
runners.spotlight: 1-7 in Irish points and 0-12 under rules; 12l third of ten in recent C&D handicap (good to soft) was, however, easily his best show in four starts for this yard, so all hope may not be lost.
runners.comment: 12l third of ten over C&D last time was his best show in four starts for this yard

runners.name: Bennys Jenny
runners.spotlight: Won an Irish mares' maiden point at the fifth attempt but has offered little in her five races under rules, pulled up on chase/handicap debut at Ayr (3m, soft; 10-1) on latest; dropped 5lb.
runners.comment: Offered little in her five races under rules, pulled up on chase/handicap debut on latest

runners.name: Lennie Godber
runners.spotlight: A whiff of ability before he flopped badly in his two handicaps (2m4f); has since changed hands and left the Twiston-Davies yard; tongue tied first time for this chase debut after four months off.
runners.comment: Flopped in both handicaps for Twiston-Davies yard; tongue tied for this chase/yard debut

races.name: Border Minstrel New Menu Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Fingals Hill
runners.spotlight: This race was one of the three he won last season, all of them on good or good to soft at this trip; on his last winning mark and, after his running-on second over 2m1f here last time, should go well again.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2025; sound second over 2m1f here last time; should go well again

runners.name: Bold Light
runners.spotlight: Three wins last season, the latest over 2m5f off a 5lb higher mark on good to soft at Kelso in March; hasn't done quite as well this term, but these conditions suit and is back on a decent mark.
runners.comment: Three wins last term; not as good this time; conditions suit, decent mark so could go well

runners.name: Haarar
runners.spotlight: Has won five of his 17 races over hurdles, including at Haydock (2m3f, good to soft) and off 7lb lower at Carlisle (2m1f, good to soft) this season; not at his best on last two runs but could bounce back.
runners.comment: 5-17 over hurdles (two wins this season); not at his best last twice but could bounce back

runners.name: Smokeringinthedark
runners.spotlight: Only win was in a four-runner Musselburgh maiden hurdle in December 2024 (2m4f, good to soft); best run this season when second over same C&D in January (good to soft); chance on that form.
runners.comment: Two best runs have been over 2m4f at Musselburgh; this ground suits; chance on best form

runners.name: Lawrenny
runners.spotlight: Won a Wetherby novice (2m, good to soft) and a Hexham handicap off 6lb lower (2m, good to soft) last season; pulled up at Bangor in November on only start this term; unproven at this trip but an interesting contender.
runners.comment: Two wins over 2m last term but pulled up only run this season (November); interesting

runners.name: Rare Edition
runners.spotlight: Creditable second in a Kelso handicap chase in October (2m1f, good); not done as well since and was well beaten back over hurdles at Kempton in January (2m, good; cheekpieces back on) on latest start; hood now tried; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Suited by good/good to soft going; well beaten back over hurdles last time; hood now tried

races.name: Border Minstrel Sunday Lunch Handicap Chase

runners.name: Ceolwulf
runners.spotlight: Won a novice handicap off 4lb lower at Warwick last March (2m4f, good); two sound runs this time, notably when second to an in-form rival at Wetherby last month (2m3f, soft); contender.
runners.comment: Warwick winner last March; good second to in-form rival at Wetherby last time; contender

runners.name: El Muchacho
runners.spotlight: Won at Catterick in January (2m3f, good) and best form since when 0.5l second at Sedgefield last time (2m5f, good); conditions suit; chance.
runners.comment: Catterick winner in January who was a close 2nd at Sedgefield last time; one with a chance
runners.owner: T J Stubbins and M E Sowersby

runners.name: Cave Hill
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over fences when scoring at Hexham last May (2m4f, good to soft); hasn't been in such good form since; 1lb lower than his winning mark; drier ground should suit.
runners.comment: Won at Hexham last May; mainly below-par since; back on a good mark; drier going suits

runners.name: Play By The Rules
runners.spotlight: Ex-Nigel Hawke; won a Taunton handicap hurdle in March 2023 (2m3f, good to soft); had a wind op at the end of that year and rather patchy form since then, including over fences; new stable in great form but he hasn't run for 516 days so perhaps best watched this time.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner for Nigel Hawke; patchy form over fences, and returns after 516-day break

runners.name: Didntgotwenty

runners.comment: Third on a couple of starts but reared as the stalls opened and lost all chance last time.

runners.name: Grand Echo
runners.spotlight: Attempted to make all when beaten a neck at Kempton (7f) last month; not in the same form at Chelmsford since and drops back in trip here.
runners.comment: Attempted to make all when beaten a neck at Kempton last month.

runners.name: Woody Y Fernandez
runners.spotlight: Placed a few times for Jennie Candlish but last of ten at Southwell (6f) in January on final run for that yard; refused to enter stalls and withdrawn from intended stable debut at Newcastle this month (9-2 at the time) when all headgear was removed (same today); might be best watched.
runners.comment: Placed a few times for Jennie Candlish but last of ten at Southwell (6f) in January.

runners.name: A Lott Of Kane
runners.spotlight: Yet to get his head in front but has run well on three occasions since switching to handicaps, latest when second of nine at Chelmsford (6f) last Thursday; a replication of that form should see him go close.
runners.comment: Yet to win but has run well on three occasions since switching to handicaps.

runners.name: Baileys Ontherocks
runners.spotlight: 0-7 for James Tate and could finish only eighth of ten on her stable debut over C&D two weeks ago; may need more help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Could finish only eighth of ten on her stable debut over C&D two weeks ago.

runners.name: Mon Petit Frere
runners.spotlight: Yet to show he can win races; first-time cheekpieces go on and he needs significant improvement.
runners.comment: Yet to show he can win races; needs significant improvement.

runners.name: Backfire
runners.spotlight: Yet to be placed from six starts and difficult to recommend.
runners.comment: Yet to be placed from six starts and difficult to recommend.

runners.name: Sequential
runners.spotlight: Has only beaten one rival in her four starts and easy to rule out.
runners.comment: Has only beaten one rival in her four starts and easy to rule out.

runners.name: Maid In Devon
runners.spotlight: Down the field on all three starts last term; makes AW/handicap debut and improvement required.
runners.comment: Down the field on all three starts last term; makes AW/handicap debut.

races.name: Ryans Cleaning Fillies Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Shirzan
runners.spotlight: Both starts at Leopardstown; beaten 16l in an Academy hurdle on debut and 11l in a bumper since; looks a big player in this company.
runners.comment: Beaten 16l in an Academy hurdle and 11l in a bumper; looks a big player in this company

runners.name: Ballyhahill
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 1m1f-1m4f Flat (RPR 84) and useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to winners Opposition (1m4f) and Dutchartcollector (1m 2yo); by a useful Flat sire but dam was a dual-purpose winner; won't need to be a star to take this on debut and the market can guide.
runners.comment: Nice pedigree; won't need to be a star to take this on debut and the market can guide
runners.owner: David Spratt & Kevin McMunigal & Mark Hopkins

runners.name: Louies Folly
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; Flat maiden has run respectably in two maiden hurdles recently and looks capable of being a big player here.
runners.comment: Respectable runs in two maiden hurdles and looks capable of being a big player here

runners.name: Hunting Gold
runners.spotlight: Some promise on debut in an Academy hurdle but a lesser effort in an open maiden since; others preferred.
runners.comment: Some promise on debut in an Academy hurdle but a lesser effort in an open maiden since

runners.name: Rebel Queen
runners.spotlight: Beaten 32l and 36l in maiden hurdles at Gowran lately; needs to find more to win but capable of being involved in a weak race.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten twice but is capable of being involved in a weak race

runners.name: Codice
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability on the Flat in France last year without winning but was poor on sole Irish run; starts over hurdles for new yard but best watched.
runners.comment: Poor on sole Irish Flat run; starts over hurdles for new yard but best watched

runners.name: Look At You
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; dam runner-up over 11.3f (RPR 65), half-sister to 1m2f Listed Flat/useful 2m hurdle winner Baron De'L and 1m2f Flat/useful 2m-2m1f hurdle winner Princeton Plains; decent pedigree and is worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Decent pedigree and is worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Shirleynightingale
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; sister to useful 2m4f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Bioluminescence, half-sister to winners The Flame (useful 3m hurdle/3m2f chase) and Dollar Bae (bumper/1m7f-2m3f hurdle); dam 2m3f-3m hurdle/chase winner; by the top jumps sire and a solid damline; the market can guide on debut.
runners.comment: By the top jumps sire and a solid damline; the market can guide on debut

runners.name: Boogie Inthejungle
runners.spotlight: A regressive Flat maiden has been well beaten both completed hurdle starts; best to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: A regressive Flat maiden has been well beaten both completed hurdle starts; look elsewhere

runners.name: Kashel Spring
runners.spotlight: Won twice on the Flat for Joseph O'Brien last year before being sold for 26,000euros; can't be fancied on the evidence of three poor hurdle runs.
runners.comment: Decent Flat winner but can't be fancied on the evidence of three poor hurdle runs

runners.name: Eitilt
runners.spotlight: Struggled in two Academy hurdles last year and enormous improvement is required.
runners.comment: Struggled in two Academy hurdles last year and enormous improvement is required

races.name: Ryans Cleaning (C & G) Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Manoir De Mirande
runners.spotlight: French bumper winner was in the lead with every chance when falling at the last in a Cork maiden hurdle on Irish debut; has been inconsistent in three starts since but is rated 123 and should win if repeating his best form.
runners.comment: Inconsistent in last three starts but is rated 123 and should win if repeating his best

runners.name: Crooked Path
runners.spotlight: Flat maiden has run with credit several times over hurdles, including twice at this track and is ground-versatile; should be closely involved.
runners.comment: Flat maiden has run with credit several times over hurdles, including here;  be involved

runners.name: Fiveandahalf
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; brother to 1m6f Flat (RPR 92) and useful bumper/2m2f hurdle winner Sixandahalf, half-brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Lake Winnipesaukee; dam 2m hurdle winner; has a decent pedigree and could be capable of a good showing on debut; check the market.
runners.comment: Decent pedigree and could be capable of a good showing on debut; check the market

runners.name: Beauvallon
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability on the Flat in France without winning; beaten 23l in a Thurles maiden hurdle on debut for this yard; has to improve plenty from that.
runners.comment: Beaten 23l in a Thurles maiden hurdle on debut for this yard; has to improve plenty

runners.name: Ivebeenkewadesert
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam unraced half-sister to useful 2m4f-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Delusionofgrandeur, out of 7f-1m2f winning half-sister to bumper/useful 2m-2m1f hurdle winner Santia; unsold for E9,500 as a 3yo; will have to be a smart sort to take this on debut but worth a market check.
runners.comment: Will have to be a smart sort to take this on debut but worth a market check

runners.name: Trev The Rev
runners.spotlight: Third foal; unraced dam out of maiden sister to useful bumper and 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase winner Uimheraceathair; unsold for E2,800 as a 3yo; best watched on debut unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Unsold for E2,800 as a 3yo; best watched on debut unless the market speaks

runners.name: Chief Josef
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three hurdle runs plus a point so has to find loads of improvement.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three hurdle runs plus a point so has to find loads of improvement

runners.name: Try Them
runners.spotlight: Flat-bred ran with credit on debut in an Academy hurdle but was well-held when falling at the last at Haydock since; best watched today.
runners.comment: Just a glimmer of promise in two hurdle runs; best watched today

runners.name: Elman
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; modest Flat sort showed some ability on hurdling debut over C&D before pulled-up at Ascot next time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Some ability on hurdling debut over C&D before pulled-up next time; others preferred

runners.name: Pilgrims Walk
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in a Cork maiden hurdle on debut and is best watched for now.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in a Cork maiden hurdle on debut and is best watched for now

runners.name: Folk Warrior
runners.spotlight: Modest in three Flat maidens and a similar story in two hurdle runs; others preferred.
runners.comment: Modest in three Flat maidens and a similar story in two hurdle runs; others preferred

races.name: Ryans Cleaning Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Thisaintthat
runners.spotlight: Ran to an RPR of 109 when fifth on debut at Punchestown (2m, sft-hvy); weakened when upped in trip at the same venue on latest; leading form claims based on that debut effort.
runners.comment: RPR of 109 on Punchestown on debut, didn't stay 2m4f latest, leading form claims

runners.name: Blazing Duke
runners.spotlight: Ran a nice race on hurdles debut at Gowran (2m, heavy) when a 9.5l fifth of 16; didn't stay when upped in trip at Naas; unseated early at Wexford two weeks ago; should be thereabouts with a repeat of that Gowran effort.
runners.comment: Ran well at Gowran, didn't stay at Naas, unseated early latest, chance on Gowran effort

runners.name: Royal Belief
runners.spotlight: Modest on the Flat and on the evidence of his three previous maiden hurdle efforts a rating of 97 might flatter him (best RPR 80); others appeal more.
runners.comment: Modest on Flat and so far over hurdles, mark of 97 may flatter him, others preferred

runners.name: Koeur A Prendre
runners.spotlight: Beaten 39l on debut at Fairyhouse and 38l at Leopardstown last time; may do better when tackling handicaps.
runners.comment: Beaten similar distances in two previous maidens, will find level in handicaps

runners.name: LAmiral Fromentin
runners.spotlight: Maiden point-to-pointer; four chase starts under Rules' and beaten 41l off a mark of 92 on handicap chase debut at Wexford latest; best watched on hurdles debut in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten off 92 in handicap chase latest, watch on hurdles debut; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Endless Talking
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner on good ground; minor promise in a Fairyhouse bumper in November; soundly beaten in another bumper at Down Royal in December; has found a weak race for his hurdling debut but testing ground is an unknown.
runners.comment: Point winner, minor promise in bumpers, weak race for hurdles debut, ground a query

runners.name: Daisy Fingall
runners.spotlight: Very modest efforts in maidens at Fairyhouse and Leopardstown just under a year apart; in good hands and well related (half-sister to Captain Guinness) but hard to make a case for on form.
runners.comment: Modest efforts in first two maiden hurdles, well bred, in good hands but hard to fancy

runners.name: Listentomewillya
runners.spotlight: Second run over this C&D wasn't devoid of promise but pulled up last time out upped to 2m4f at Fairyhouse when 125-1; a repeat of her C&D effort may be enough to challenge for a place in this company.
runners.comment: Pulled up latest, not a bad C&D run on penultimate start, could place with a repeat

runners.name: Brackens Jet
runners.spotlight: 150-1 for both starts; Naas seventh on latest was a slightly better effort but was still beaten 41l; will qualify for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: 150-1 for both starts, beaten 41l at Naas latest, qualifies for handicaps after this

runners.name: Jetstream Jeff
runners.spotlight: Yet to get closer than 82l to the winner in three previous maiden hurdle runs; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats in three maiden hurdles, seems safe to rule out

runners.name: Any Chance
runners.spotlight: Triple figure odds and ran accordingly in both previous starts; handicaps beckon.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats at huge odds both starts, handicaps beckon

runners.name: El Phenomeno
runners.spotlight: Hasn't cut much ice in maidens or in first four handicaps, although his Cork fifth wasn't a terrible run; rated just 75 and the new cheekpieces need to have a dramatic effect.
runners.comment: 0-8, rated just 75, new cheekpieces need to have a dramatic effect

races.name: Party Time Mares Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Chemdawg
runners.spotlight: Couple of placed efforts in November-December but hasn't gone with any enthusiasm the last twice, pulling up at Clonmel and Down Royal; needs to get back on track; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Placed twice in Nov-Dec but pulled up the last twice, needs to get back on track

runners.name: Westernersunrise
runners.spotlight: Won off 3lb lower at Fairyhouse in February last year; promising seasonal return at Limerick hasn't been built on and failed to complete two of last three; new headgear combination needs to spark.
runners.comment: Failed to complete two of last three starts, new headgear combo needs to spark

runners.name: Nashville Notions
runners.spotlight: Won a mares' maiden hurdle at Sligo in June (17.5f, good); failed to complete the last twice but was creeping into the race when unseating four out at Cork last time; might prefer better ground than this.
runners.comment: Sligo maiden hurdle winner, unseated when making headway latest, ground a query

runners.name: Classy Cc
runners.spotlight: Maiden has placed on two of her last three starts; beaten 3.5l when second of 17 at Naas (2m, sft-hvy) on latest; raised 2lb but should be in the mix again if seeing out this longer trip (dam a half-sister to a 3m winner).
runners.comment: Placed in two of last three, beaten 3.5l latest at Naas, up 2lb, in the mix if stays

runners.name: Elsannah
runners.spotlight: Dual winner for Shane Crawley at around 3m; should strip fitter for her return from a mammoth absence at Navan 10 days ago; this trip may be on the sharp side though.
runners.comment: Strip fitter for comeback run at Navan, this trip may just be on the sharp side

runners.name: Lovely Reaction
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner has failed to complete all four starts since returning from a 496 day layoff; on last winning mark now but a risky proposition.
runners.comment: Failed to complete all four starts since return from layoff, risky proposition

runners.name: Roccabaker
runners.spotlight: Gained first win at Tramore on New Year's Day (21.5f, soft) off 8lb lower; beaten 10.5l off revised mark at Punchestown and pulled up on latest at Clonmel; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Won at Tramore in January, beaten off revised mark and pulled up latest, revival needed

runners.name: Dolly Watkins
runners.spotlight: Getting the hang of things in handicaps; 8l third over C&D (good) on final start of 2025 and close second at Down Royal (20.5f, yielding) on return from 341 day break; up 4lb and softer ground here; obvious chance if she handles it (half-sister won on heavy).
runners.comment: Getting the hang of things, close second latest, up 4lb, very soft ground a query

runners.name: Shellrunforbriggs
runners.spotlight: Naas maiden hurdle winner; well beaten off this mark on handicap hurdle debut at Navan; last two starts have been over fences and while her Thurles listed novice chase second last time was remote, she did achieve an RPR of 110; can't discount back in handicap company.
runners.comment: Naas maiden winner, remote listed second over fences latest, can't be discounted

runners.name: Millie B
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner in point-to-points but yet to trouble the judge in five starts under Rules'; beaten cumulative distance of 81l in first two handicaps; much more needed.
runners.comment: Point winner, 0-5 under Rules', heavy defeats first two h'caps, much more needed

runners.name: Annie Button
runners.spotlight: Left all previous form behind when narrowly denied by Walk In The Clouds at Naas (2m6, sft-hvy) on latest; 4lb better off with that rival now so may be able to turn the tables with a repeat.
runners.comment: Narrowly denied by Walk In The Clouds latest, 4lb better off, could turn the tables

runners.name: Westandtogether
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on handicap debut at Navan early last month (2m6f, heavy); raised 8lb for that and given how well she saw out the trip, this drop back in distance doesn't look an obvious plus.
runners.comment: Off the mark on handicap debut latest (2m6f), up 8lb, drop in trip not an obvious plus

runners.name: Rebel Cowgirl
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 15th attempt at Punchestown last month (2m4f, soft), making all and scoring by 0.5l; this slightly shorter trip fine but raised 8lb and this is a tougher race; should still make a bold bid, however.
runners.comment: Made all at Punchestown latest, up 8lb, this harder but should make a bold bid again

runners.name: Cooltubrid Eva
runners.spotlight: Went close here on debut but has failed to back that up; beaten over 20l the last twice in handicaps here and at Clonmel; mark sliding but she's hard to fancy on recent showings.
runners.comment: Has failed to build on promising debut, beaten over 20l last twice, hard to fancy

runners.name: Peppers Corner
runners.spotlight: Ability in point-to-points but yet to replicate that under Rules; heavy defeats at big odds in all four maiden hurdles at 2m-2m3f; could improve for the switch to a handicap but can only watch for now.
runners.comment: Point winner, no show in four maiden hurdles, can only watch in first handicap

runners.name: Walk In The Clouds
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; attracted market support and shed the maiden tag over hurdles in a 0-100 2m6f mares' handicap at Naas (sft-hvy) earlier this month (Annie Button second) in first-time cheekpieces (retained); raised 7lb, drops back in trip and moves up in grade but should be competitive again.
runners.comment: Up 7lb for Naas win on latest, down in trip and this tougher but should remain competitive

races.name: Vision Contracting Handicap Chase

runners.name: Duffys Hodey
runners.spotlight: Three wins last year included a win over fences here over 2m6f; has to rebound from two poor runs since a second at Tramore on New Year's Day.
runners.comment: Course winner has to rebound from two poor runs since a second at Tramore in January

runners.name: Sophia Rose
runners.spotlight: Sligo maiden hurdle winner last August has run some fine races in defeat over fences since; had excuses for a poor run at Navan last time and the cheekpieces worn then are left off; has run well here before; chance.
runners.comment: Excuses for a poor run at Navan last time; has run well here before; chance

runners.name: Hello Jack
runners.spotlight: Maiden over hurdles and fences but has shown some ability; disappointed at Leopardstown last time in cheekpieces which are left on; the drop in trip today may help so considered.
runners.comment: Disappointed at Leopardstown last time in cheekpieces; the drop in trip today may help

runners.name: Way Down South
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle at this track in 2024; got off the mark over fences in a Cork beginners' last time in first-time tongue-tie/cheekpieces; the third home has franked the form since; chance.
runners.comment: Won a Cork beginners' last time in first-time tongue-tie/cheekpieces; form franked; chance

runners.name: Donnrua Dream
runners.spotlight: Two of his three hurdle wins have come at this track with the latest last month and not a bad effort back here last time; hasn't run over fences in a while but would have a squeak on his best efforts.
runners.comment: Runs well here; hasn't run over fences in a while but would have a squeak on his best form

runners.name: Shanroe Act
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles and fences within five days in January but was pulled-up over hurdles since; this same mark looks stiff back over fences.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles and fences within five days in January; this chase mark looks stiff

runners.name: Teescomponentsyess
runners.spotlight: Has won twice over hurdles at this track but has been poor over hurdles and fences lately having returned from an absence; others preferred.
runners.comment: Poor over hurdles and fences lately having returned from an absence; others preferred

runners.name: Another Ocana
runners.spotlight: Has a win apiece over hurdles and fences at this track; has been soundly beaten in both codes back here the last twice but can do better.
runners.comment: Has been soundly beaten in both codes back here the last twice but can do better

runners.name: Midnight Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Kilbeggan maiden hurdle winner last year and has run fine races in defeat over fences since; has been freshened up since last start and can be involved if ready enough.
runners.comment: Consistent sort has been freshened up since last start and can be involved if ready enough

runners.name: Knockbrack Rambler
runners.spotlight: One of his two hurdle wins came at this track; has been soundly beaten in four handicap chases lately and needs to do better.
runners.comment: Has been soundly beaten in four handicap chases lately and needs to do better

runners.name: Malbas
runners.spotlight: Triple Flat winner won twice over hurdles last year at 2m but was disappointing on two of last three starts; chasing debut after a break and others preferred.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner makes chasing debut after a break and others preferred

runners.name: Syracus Du Houx
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability without winning over hurdles in 2024; was beaten a long way on return at Gowran last time and has to improve hugely on that for chasing debut.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on return at Gowran last time; has to improve hugely for chasing debut

races.name: Vision Contracting Handicap Chase

runners.name: Mount Frisco
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles at Clonmel and fences at Gowran last month but disappointed here over fences last time so has to rebound; claims if he does.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles and fences last month but disappointed here last time and has to rebound

runners.name: Marelly
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; course hurdle winner won at Gowran in January and ran well here last time; was poor when last seen over fences though, so questions to answer in that respect.
runners.comment: Course hurdle winner ran well here last time; was poor when last seen over fences though

runners.name: Quest With Speed
runners.spotlight: In good form mostly over hurdles during winter, winning at Navan and a decent effort last time; was pulled-up on last chase start in the Cork National, though, and this trip looks on the sharp side.
runners.comment: In good form mostly over hurdles during winter but pulled-up on last chase start

runners.name: Come On The Lads
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler won a valuable prize at Galway last year; ran well at Fairyhouse on the first of two chase starts last autumn; one to consider on return and the market can guide as to readiness.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler ran well on the first of two chase starts last autumn; one to consider

runners.name: Spinning Web
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner at Thurles last year over 2m5f; pulled-up here on final 2025 start and fell at the first on return lately at Navan; seems best watched for now.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner fell at first on return lately and is best watched for now

runners.name: Its Not Over Yet
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins in 2024 and some good efforts in defeat last year; makes her chase debut after a break and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Two hurdle wins; makes chase debut after a break and others make more appeal

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares INH Flat Race

runners.name: Araminta
runners.spotlight: Runner up in two of her three starts and ran with credit when a 3.75l fourth in a Grade 2 bumper at Leopardstown in February; sets the standard on form and should be thereabouts if staying this longer trip.
runners.comment: Runner up two of three starts, good run in Grade 2 last month, sets the standard

runners.name: Pets Georgina
runners.spotlight: Order Of St George mare; dam bumper/2m4f hurdle winner, half-sister to smart bumper and 2m6f-3m hurdle/chase winner A Genie In Abottle, out of bumper winning half-sister to 3m1f chase winner Skeheenarinky; nicely bred and needs checking in the market.
runners.comment: Order Of St George mare, dam bumper winner, nicely bred, check the market

runners.name: Thelittleballerina
runners.spotlight: Weakened late on and hung left when a 3.75l third of 13 in a mares' bumper at Down Royal (17.5f, yielding) on debut two weeks ago; should have learnt plenty from that experience and bred to stay this far; in the mix.
runners.comment: Should have learnt plenty from promising debut run at Down Royal, in the mix

runners.name: Ronnie Returns
runners.spotlight: 66-1 and tailed off on debut at Gowran earlier this month; huge step forward needed to be competitive here.
runners.comment: 66-1 and tailed off on debut at Gowran, huge step forward needed here

runners.name: Sapphire Jewel
runners.spotlight: Walk In The Park 4yo; sister to bumper/2m4f hurdle winner Frankie John, half-sister to useful winners Shearer (bumper and 2m4f-3m1f hurdle/chase) and I Am Lorenzo (2m chase); dam unraced half-sister to high-class Empire Of Dirt; yard 1-27 in bumpers last five seasons; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Walk In the Park 4yo, sister to bumper winner, yard 1-27 in this sphere last 5 seasons

runners.name: Dream Frequency
runners.spotlight: Casamento mare; half-sister to four winners inc Isabella Bee (2m-2m3f hurdle/chase), Diamond Koda (2m3f hurdle) and Fleur De La Roque (French 2m1f hurdle); dam useful 1m4f Flat/2m1f hurdle winner; yard may have better chance with Shandonagh.
runners.comment: Casamento mare, dam useful Flat and hurdle winner, Shandonagh looks yard's main hope

runners.name: Even Tho
runners.spotlight: Crystal Ocean mare fell when had the race at her mercy in sole point-to-point start at Dromahane; the winner of that race has since won a bumper by 19l at Warwick; half-sister to useful bumper and 2m4f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner The Enabler from the family of Denman; has joined top yard and obvious chance.
runners.comment: Crystal Ocean mare, fell with race at mercy at Dromahane, form franked, obvious chance

runners.name: Lady Du Plessis
runners.spotlight: Cokoriko mare; third foal; half-sister to useful 2m5f/2m6f hurdle winner Kamikaz Du Plessis; dam French 1m7f AQPS Flat winner, half-sister to French 1m4f AQPS winner Duc Du Plessis; yard 1-21 in bumpers last five seasons; watch.
runners.comment: Cokoriko mare, dam French bumper winner, yard 1-21 in bumpers last 5 seasons

runners.name: Royal Tiara
runners.spotlight: Getaway mare; E17,000 foal; half-sister to winners Rioga Choice (useful 2m hurdle), Its Gonnahappen (2m hurdle) and Special John (3m/3m2f chase); dam 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner; market best guide on debut.
runners.comment: Getaway mare, half-sister to useful Rioga Choice, market best guide on debut

runners.name: Shadonagh
runners.spotlight: 16-1 and ran a decent race on debut at Leopardstown (2m, soft), staying on nicely enough after getting outpaced; has 11l to find with Araminta on that effort but could get much closer now stepped up in trip; definite place chance.
runners.comment: Stayed on nicely when behind Araminta at Leopardstown, longer trip a plus, place shout

runners.name: Ash Hobbs
runners.spotlight: Form of her point-to-point win hasn't worked out and she was beaten 33l when fifth of 17 in a Fairyhouse bumper 108 days ago; in good hands and open to improvement but much more needed.
runners.comment: Point win hasn't worked out, beaten 33l on Rules' debut, much more needed

runners.name: Madam Jess
runners.spotlight: Best of four runs under Rules' came on good ground in a bumper at Punchestown in October 2024; back from a long layoff with a modest effort in a Punchestown bumper on New Year's Eve; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Best effort on good ground, well beaten after absence latest, others preferred

runners.name: Nells Princess
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner ran a pleasing race in a Punchestown bumper on New Year's Eve (Madam Jess behind); place chance if she can repeat that.
runners.comment: Point winner, pleasing run at Punchestown latest, place chance with a repeat
